entries.title: Bears GM Ryan Poles' son was furious he dealt DJ Moore
entries.summary: <p>Bears general manager Ryan Poles’ son Mason was not happy he traded DJ Moore. </p><p>His extended family — Poles is from Western New York — was thrilled he dealt the receiver to their beloved Bills.</p><p>There’s nothing the GM could do about either reaction — though Moore told the younger Poles to show his dad some mercy.</p><p>“It was cool DJ sent him a video telling him not to be mad at dad — ‘It’s part of the business,’” Poles said Thursday.</p><p>The GM has often called Moore one of his favorite players. Two years ago, he gave Moore a four-year, $110 million extension that technically kicked in Wednesday. How, then, did Moore go from a building block to trade bait — he landed the Bears a second-round pick — so quickly?</p><p>The short answer: Luther Burden happened. The Bears drafted the Missouri receiver 39<sup>th</sup> overall last year only after Ohio State running back TreVeyon Henderson was taken one spot ahead of him. The Bears thought he’d be drafted much earlier.</p><p>“That’s why you follow the draft board,” Poles said. “I did not expect Luther Burden to be where he was, and he showed up and showed some big-time flashes. Every time that dude touches the ball, special things can happen.”</p><p>Having tight end Colston Loveland and receiver Rome Odunze, first-round picks the last two years, gave Poles another reason to move Moore rather than pay his $24.5 million cap hit this year.</p><p>“There’s a financial piece of it,” Poles said, “but you’re also looking at the room.”</p><h3><b>Notes</b></h3><p>• Tackle Jedrick Wills, who signed with the Bears on Thursday, spent last year out of football to recover from a leg injury.<br>“Changed my perspective on the game,” he said. “I feel like I missed it more than usual.”<br>Poles is excited to put the former first-round pick under the tutelage of his offensive line coaches.<br>“We think there is a connection there between our coaches and him,” he said.<br>• Poles called Drew Dalman’s retirement unexpected but said he’d leave the public explanation to the center.<br>• The Bears re-signed guard Jordan McFadden, who started in the playoff loss to the Rams.<br>• Former Bears tight end Durham Smythe signed a one-year deal to rejoin former offensive coordinator Declan Doyle in Baltimore.<br>• Poles was visibly frustrated when asked about the NFL’s decision to not give the Bears two third-round draft picks as compensation for losing assistant GM Ian Cunningham to the Falcons — but otherwise declined comment.</p>
entries.author: Patrick Finley

entries.title: Bears transaction tracker: Who's coming, going this offseason
entries.summary: <p>The <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears" target="_blank" >Bears</a> have been busy with a flurry of moves as they try to sharpen their roster — and make prudent salary cap decisions. </p><p>They can start agreeing to deals with free agents Monday, though players can't officially sign their contracts until the start of the league year Wednesday.</p><p>Here's a look at who the Bears have gained and lost: </p><h3>March 12</h3><p>Former Bears safety <b>Jaquan Brisker</b> agreed to a one-year deal to <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/12/bears-steelers-safety-jaquan-brisker-heading-home-pittsburgh-nfl-free-agency" target="_blank" >play for his hometown Steelers.</a></p><p>Safety-turned-cornerback <b>C.J. Gardner-Johnson</b>, who played well over 10 games after joining the Bears midseason, <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/12/bears-steelers-safety-jaquan-brisker-heading-home-pittsburgh-nfl-free-agency" target="_blank" >is signing with the Bills</a>.</p><p>Former Bears tight end <b>Durham Smythe</b> signed a one-year deal to rejoin former offensive coordinator Declan Doyle in Baltimore.</p><p>Guard <b>Jordan McFadden</b>, who started in the Bears' playoff loss to the Rams, is returning to the team on a one-year deal. McFadden played 13 games and started twice for the Chargers from 2023-24.</p><h3>March 11</h3><p>Former Browns left tackle <b>Jedrick Wills</b> will join the Bears' starting competition. He'll sign a one-year deal, a source confirmed to the Sun-Times. Wills is a former No. 10 overall pick who started 45 games for the Browns from 2020-22 before being limited by injury the next two years. He sat out last year because of a knee injury. </p><p>Former Bears safety <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/11/bears-star-safety-kevin-byard-to-sign-with-patriots-report" target="_blank" >Kevin Byard</a> is signing a one-year deal with the Patriots to reunite with former Titans coach Mike Vrabel. The Bears said this offseason they'd hope to work out a deal with their team captain and Pro Bowl player but seemed to move on when they gave Coby Bryant a three-year, $40 million deal Monday.</p><p>The Bears agreed to re-sign backup safety <b>Elijah Hicks</b> on a one-year contract Wednesday, a source said. He was a seventh-round pick in general manager Ryan Poles' first draft class in 2022 and has played 61 games (15 starts) for the Bears.</p><p>Former Bears defensive lineman <b>Chris Williams</b> jumped to the Falcons, who now are run by general manager Ian Cunningham, on a one-year, $2.4 million deal, ESPN reported.</p><h3>March 10</h3><p>The Bears added to their defensive line depth Tuesday when they agreed to sign <b>Kentavius Street</b> to a one-year deal, a source confirmed to the Sun-Times. He's played for four teams in his seven years in the league.</p><p>Street spent 2022 playing for the Saints, where Bears defensive coordinator Dennis Allen served as head coach. He posted a career-high 3 ½ sacks that season and has only 10 ½ in his career. Pro Football Focus rated him as the 122<sup>nd</sup>-best run defender among the 224 eligible interior defensive linemen in their system last year. He spent a career-high 104 snaps over tackle in 2022, his year playing for Allen.</p><p>The Bears are signing former Bills defensive back <b>Cam Lewis</b> to a two-year deal, a source confirmed to the Sun-Times on Tuesday.</p><p>Like Coby Bryant, whom the Bears signed a day earlier, Lewis has experience at cornerback, safety and in the slot.</p><p>Lewis, who turns 29 next month, went to college at Buffalo and then spent the first six years of his career with the Bills. He started only 14 games, but 10 of those came over the past two years. Lewis played 38% of the Bills defensive snaps but was a special teams stalwart, playing on 51% of their kicking downs. He’s played at least 51% of his team’s special teams snaps in each of the past four years.</p><p>Former Bears cornerback <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/10/bears-jets-nahshon-wright-agrees-free-agent-deal-interceptions-fumble-receovery-forced-nfl" ><b>Nahshon Wright </b></a>agreed to a one-year, $5.5 million deal with the Jets, per NFL Network. Wright had five interceptions and started all but one game for the Bears last season, one year after the Vikings kept him on their practice squad for most of the season.</p><p> <br>The Bears agreed to a one-year, $5.1 million deal with former Lions wide receiver and return man <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/10/bears-to-sign-former-lions-wide-receiver-returner-kalif-raymond-source" ><b>Kalif Raymond</b></a>. He had 24 catches for 289 yards and a touchdown for the Lions last season.</p><h3>March 9</h3><p>Third-string quarterback <b>Case Keenum</b>, who was a key part of Caleb Williams’ development last year under coach Ben Johnson, is coming back to the Bears, a source confirmed. He’ll make as $5.5 million and as much as $8 million over two years, per ESPN.</p><p>Keenum hasn't thrown a pass since 2023 but has been part of the growth of both Williams and Texans quarterback C.J. Stroud late in his career.</p><p>“He has been an integral part of what we’ve done,” Johnson said in January, days after the Bears’ emotional playoff win against the Packers. “He has been tremendous in terms of Caleb’s growth. That was really the thought process of bringing him in, is: the guy with skins on the wall, he has played in big games himself on a high level.</p><p>"He has won big games, he has lost big games ... it’s something that all these young players can really lean into, particularly in weeks like this where you come off an emotional win and yet you got to turn your sights on the next page pretty quickly."</p><p>After one of the best seasons of his career, former <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears" target="_blank" >Bears</a> linebacker <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/09/former-bears-linebacker-tremaine-edmunds-gets-3-year-36-million-deal-from-giants-report" ><b>Tremaine Edmunds</b></a> got a big contract offer from the Giants. He agreed Monday to a three-year, $36 million deal, sources confirmed to the Sun-Times. It includes $24 million guaranteed.</p><p>Former Bears defensive end <b>Dominique Robinson</b>, a fifth-round pick in 2022, is signing with the Texans on a one-year, $4 milliion deal, ESPN reported.</p><p>The Bears will sign linebacker <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/09/bears-to-sign-playmaking-veteran-devin-bush-jr-as-they-rebuild-linebacker-corps-source" ><b>Devin Bush Jr.</b> </a>from the Browns to a three-year, $30 million deal after the departure of Edmunds, a source said. Bush, a former No. 10 overall pick, had three interceptions and two forced fumbles last season.</p><p>Former Colts defensive tackle <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/09/bears-first-move-in-free-agency-is-deal-with-dt-neville-gallimore-source" ><b>Neville Gallimore</b></a> is joining the Bears on a two-year, $12 million deal when the new league year opens, a source said. He played all 17 games in Indianapolis last season, including eight starts.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" > <div class="TweetUrl"> <blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">Bears Nation let’s do it!!!! Thank you for the opportunity. Let’s work! <a href="https://twitter.com/hashtag/BearDown?src=hash&ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">#BearDown</a> 🐻</p>— Neville Gallimore (@Path2Greatwork) <a href="https://twitter.com/Path2Greatwork/status/2031059927179972657?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">March 9, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script> </div> </div> </div></div><p>Linebacker <b>D'Marco Jackson</b> is returning to the Bears on a two-year, $7.5 million deal, a source confirmed. The Bears claimed Jackson on cut day during training camp, citing his experience in coordinator Dennis Allen's system while in New Orleans. He proved to be a solid special teams player and a worthy spot starter. In Week 15, he was named NFC Defensive Player of the Week after logging the first sack and interception of his career in a win against the Browns. </p><p>Receiver <b>Olamide Zaccheaus</b> is leaving the Bears for Atlanta, a source confirmed, agreeing to sign a contract with the Falcons. Zaccheaus spent one year in Chicago after helping the Commanders develop quarterback Jayden Daniels. In Chicago, he struggled with drops — his six were tied for 13<sup>th</sup>-most in the NFL — and by the end of the season was behind DJ Moore, Rome Odunze and Luther Burden on the Bears’ depth chart. He finished with 30 catches for 315 yards. In Atlanta, he’ll be reunited with new general manager Ian Cunningham, who was the Bears’ assistant GM last season. Zaccheaus played in Atlanta from 2019-22, catching passes from new president of football Matt Ryan, the former quarterback.</p><p>With none of their top four safeties under contract for this upcoming season, the Bears needed to move quickly to grab capable players at the position. They found that and more when they <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/09/bears-sign-seahawks-coby-bryant-safety-nfl" target="_blank" >agreed to sign</a> the Seahawks' <b>Coby Bryant</b>. He'll get a three-year, $40 million deal, per ESPN. Bryant, who had four interceptions last year, can play both free safety and strong safety. It's unclear if his additions means Kevin Byard's Bears tenure is over. Both sides had been optimistic about getting a deal done for Byard, who mostly played free safety.</p><h3><b>March 8</b></h3><p>Defensive end <b>Daniel Hardy</b>, who has been a special teams ace, agreed to sign a two-year deal worth as much as $6 million to return to the Bears, a source confirmed to the Sun-Times on Sunday. Hardy played only 5% of the Bears' defensive snaps last year but appeared in a whopping 78% of their kicking plays, totaling 22 tackles.</p><h3><b>March 6</b></h3><p>The day <b>Drew Dalman</b> put in his retirement paperwork, the Bears traded a 2027 fifth-round draft pick to the Patriots for his replacement, center <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/06/bears-trade-garrett-bradbury-patriots-center-2027-fifth-round-pick-nfl" target="_blank" ><b>Garrett Bradbury</b></a>. Bradbury, who started every game for the AFC champions last year, has one year left on a $9.6 million contract he signed last year after spending six years with the Vikings.</p><h3><b>March 5</b></h3><p>Needing to clear cap space and cognizant of their depth at wide receiver, <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/halas-intrigue/2026/03/05/halas-intrigue-podcast-breaking-down-bears-dj-moore-trade-nfl" target="_blank" >the Bears traded</a> <b>DJ Moore</b> and a fifth-round pick to the Bills for a second-round draft pick this year. Moore jad just 682 receiving yards on 50 catches last year, the worst numbers of his career. Had the Bears kept Moore, he would have cost a $24.5 million salary cap hit, the third-highest on the team.</p><p>Eight days after telling linebacker <b>Tremaine Edmunds</b> and his agent that they could seek a trade, the Bears told Edmunds <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/03/05/bears-release-tremaine-edmunds-linebacker-salary-cap-nfl" target="_blank" >they were cutting him</a>. They saved $15 million in cap space in the process. Edmunds had four interceptions in 13 games last year, the second-most among all NFL linebackers.</p><h3><b>March 3</b></h3><p>Center <b>Drew Dalman</b>, who played every snap and reached the Pro Bowl in his first year with the Bears, told the team he planned to retire after just five years in the NFL. He filed his retirement paperwork three days later.</p><p></p><p></p><p></p><p></p><p></p><bsp-list-loadmore class="PageListH" data-module data-hide-authors="true" data-hide-categories="true" entries.title: Let the debate begin: Patrick Williams or Leonard Miller for the Bulls?
entries.summary: <p>LOS ANGELES — A blind résumé comparison doesn’t do Patrick Williams any favors these days. But with just 16 games remaining after the Bulls played the Lakers on Thursday, what the heck:</p><p>• Player A: In his last four games with meaningful minutes, he has averaged 12 points and seven rebounds, including a 17-point, 11-rebound performance Tuesday against Golden State.</p><p>• Player B: In his last four games, he has averaged eight points and 1.3 rebounds, and the last time he put up anything close to a 17-11 showing was Game 4 against the Bucks in the 2022 playoffs in which he scored 20 points and grabbed 10 rebounds.</p><p>That’s why it’s not crazy to wonder if little-known Leonard Miller (Player A) is a better option than Williams moving forward for not only the rest of this season but also beyond.</p><p>“Lenny is a monster,” teammate Matas Buzelis said after the Bulls’ overtime victory against the Warriors.</p><p>Miller, a 6-10 forward, was a force in that game, and six of his 11 of his rebounds were offensive. Williams’ tenacity on the offensive glass is one of the ongoing criticisms of him in Year 6 since the Bulls drafted him fourth overall in 2020. Too many lethargic games, not enough urgency.</p><p>There’s an even bigger indictment in comparing the players. Since joining the Bulls (along with Rob Dillingham and second-round picks) in the trade with the Timberwolves for Ayo Dosunmu and Julian Phillips, Miller has posted a team-high plus-7.5 per game in plus/minus in 10 games. Meanwhile, Williams is near the bottom of the team with a minus-4.8 per game.</p><p>An unfair sample size? Maybe, but the fact that the Bulls lost seven of the 10 games Miller played, and yet he still has produced winning basketball whenever he’s on the floor speaks volumes.</p><p>“With Minnesota I wasn’t playing so I kind of got used to it, but with me being used to it I’m still ready, I’m still locked in,” Miller said. “I’m always ready to go out there and compete at a high level. I think I demonstrated that and that’s what I’m going to continue to demonstrate. Me playing at a high level and having a good game, I’m kind of not surprised because I have that belief in myself, I’m confident, and I feel like the sky is the limit with me.”</p><p>Will the Bulls agree?</p><p>After Miller played solid minutes against the Suns last Thursday, he only got a minute of mop-up time in the next game against the Kings. Williams returned from an injury and played 17 minutes in that loss. But Williams tweaked his ankle, and that’s why Miller got the start against the Warriors.</p><p>And he made it count.</p><p>“He’s very active on the glass, he’s a good runner in transition, he’s a very active body as far as cutting and moving,” coach Billy Donovan said. “I just like his activity and his motor. There’s certain things he’s inexperienced at that will come in time, but overall, his activity, he can just get a lot of things into the game.”</p><p>Williams was out against the Lakers, but what will Donovan do when he has both forwards healthy again? That’s the question.</p><p>It didn’t sound like Miller was concerned.</p><p>“It’s all about staying ready, it’s all about staying locked in,” Miller said. “I pay attention to the game plan. My focus is coming in and making winning plays and that’s all I care about.”</p><p>One more blind comparison:</p><p>• Player A: Has a $2.4 million team option for next season.</p><p>• Player B: Guaranteed $18 million a year through the 2028-29 season.</p><p>No one said comparisons were fair.<br></p>
entries.author: Joe Cowley

entries.title: Sixth grader wins CPS spelling bee
entries.summary: <p>Less studying, better results. That was Seraj Zerhouni's strategy at the Chicago Public Schools Spelling Bee Championship on Thursday afternoon. </p><p>Despite months of studying difficult words, Seraj lost last year's citywide spelling bee by misspelling "Tiffany." The LaSalle II Magnet School sixth grader returned this year with a simpler approach — prepare a little less and study easier words. </p><p>It worked. Over about two-and-a-half hours, Seraj advanced steadily through the early rounds, then won the championship in Round 18.</p><p>In May, he will compete at the Scripps National Spelling Bee in Washington, D.C. </p><p>"It feels like redemption," Seraj said. "I was pretty disappointed last year that I didn't get so far, and now I'm, like, determined to get far, and then I got far."</p><p>Seraj took the title after correctly spelling "logothete," which means an administrator in the Byzantine Empire. He had outlasted the other 48 CPS students from grades 1 through 8 gathered at Alexander Graham Bell Elementary School, 3730 N. Oakley Ave.</p><p>When Seraj approaches a new word, he said he looks at its roots to find patterns, then asks for the definition. Thursday, he repeatedly asked the moderator for clarification to ensure he was identifying the right word. For a word like "logothete," with Greek origins, he said this was important. </p><p>"There's so many times where people, they think they have a word, but it's actually a different word. They spell that word, and they end up getting it wrong, through, you know, misconception," Seraj said. </p><p>Though Seraj focused less on studying ahead of this year's spelling bee, he said when he did choose to study, he used an app and physical lists. With the help of his parents, he spelled words over and over again to master them. </p><p>"I can't spell at all, like even basic words," Seraj's mother, Anna Lusero said. "I'm amazed at his hard work, his determination, and his learning."</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-940000" name="image-940000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/bf3690f/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4128x2317+0+218/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc7%2F24%2F95ea17604be2b8efb3e9dcf3e794%2Fspellingbee-260313-04.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/3f6574f/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4128x2317+0+218/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc7%2F24%2F95ea17604be2b8efb3e9dcf3e794%2Fspellingbee-260313-04.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/61b6b4b/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4128x2317+0+218/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc7%2F24%2F95ea17604be2b8efb3e9dcf3e794%2Fspellingbee-260313-04.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="Chicago-area students take part in the Chicago Public Schools Citywide Spelling Bee Championship at Alexander Graham Bell Elementary School on Thursday, March 12 at 3730 N. Oakley Ave." srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/61b6b4b/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4128x2317+0+218/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc7%2F24%2F95ea17604be2b8efb3e9dcf3e794%2Fspellingbee-260313-04.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/0918d91/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4128x2317+0+218/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc7%2F24%2F95ea17604be2b8efb3e9dcf3e794%2Fspellingbee-260313-04.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/61b6b4b/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4128x2317+0+218/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc7%2F24%2F95ea17604be2b8efb3e9dcf3e794%2Fspellingbee-260313-04.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Chicago-area students take part in the Chicago Public Schools Citywide Spelling Bee Championship on Thursday at Alexander Graham Bell Elementary School, 3730 N. Oakley Ave.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Timothy Hiatt/For the Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>Ahead of the national competition in May, Lusero said she's excited for people to learn more about her son, who is, she said, "pretty awesome."</p><p>Throughout the competition Seraj and second-place finisher Damien Kennedy, a fifth grader at Brennemann Elementary School, exchanged high fives and fist bumps as they advanced. Before Thursday, they were strangers. By the end, they celebrated the moment as friends. </p><p>Damien said throughout the competition, the two believed in each other, which helped them become the two final spellers. Damien was eliminated by misspelling "concours" — defined as a "public competition." Despite the outcome, Damien said: "You can't be upset at every loss."</p><p>"I always try to find friendship and bonds wherever I am, especially at the spelling bee in high-stakes, nerve-wracking experiences," Seraj said. "I try to be friendly with people, develop connections and that just makes it a little better."</p><p>Seraj said his trophy will go on a shelf next to his favorite PEZ candy dispensers, photos and books — and a Funko Pop! of Albert Einstein.</p><p>After his comeback Thursday, Seraj said he hopes his story shows "anyone can go back and do something, anything that they want, if they're determined, they work hard, and they really try to get it."</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-200000" name="image-200000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/705996c/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4128x2317+0+218/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F69%2F8c%2Fb2686b474bf8916510322721bed8%2Fspellingbee-260313-06.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/47f4471/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4128x2317+0+218/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F69%2F8c%2Fb2686b474bf8916510322721bed8%2Fspellingbee-260313-06.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/176ac5b/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4128x2317+0+218/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F69%2F8c%2Fb2686b474bf8916510322721bed8%2Fspellingbee-260313-06.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="The panel of judges at the Chicago Public Schools Citywide Spelling Bee Championship at Alexander Graham Bell Elementary School, 3730 N. entries.author: Elleiana Green

entries.title: Big Ten tournament: Northwestern can't handle Purdue in 81-68 third-round loss
entries.summary: <p><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/college-sports/2026/03/10/big-ten-tournament-opening-night-northwestern-penn-state-indiana-chris-collins-nick-martinelli" target="_blank" >Beating lowly Penn State</a> was one thing. <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/college-sports/2026/03/11/northwestern-wildcats-indiana-hoosiers-big-ten-tournament-purdue-march-madness" target="_blank" >Getting the better of Indiana</a> was something to feel pretty good about.</p><p>But going up against taller, more talented, just plain better Purdue was another matter altogether for Northwestern, which lost to the Boilermakers 81-68 in the third round of the <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/college-sports" target="_blank" >Big Ten men’s basketball</a> tournament at the United Center.</p><p>That’s all she wrote for the Wildcats, who finished with a win total — only 15 in 34 games — that matched their No. 15 tournament seed.</p><p>“It’s tough to be at the end of the road for us, man,” coach Chris Collins said. “It always sucks. It always sucks. You’re in the fight, you’re in the fight, and then it’s over.”</p><p>It wasn’t much of a fight in this one until it was too late. Perhaps weary from their first two games, the Wildcats were dropped to the canvas early and trailed 45-21 at the half. For what it’s worth, they outscored the Boilermakers 47-36 in the second half.</p><p>Collins watched senior Nick Martinelli, one of the best players he’s had in 13 years atop the program, go out with 25 points in 39 minutes, bringing his school single-season scoring record to 759 points.</p><p>“Hopefully every person who played with me, they just knew a guy who worked relentlessly,” Martinelli said.</p><p>The Wildcats had no answers for Boilers big men Trey Kaufman-Renn and Oscar Cluff, who scored 19 each and missed only four shots between them, or the elite playmaking of point guard Braden Smith, who piled up 16 assists.</p><p>Seventh-seeded Purdue (24-8) will meet second-seeded Nebraska on Friday.</p>
entries.author: Steve Greenberg

entries.title: Reggie Postlewaite, Peoria Manual refuse to lose and earn a spot in 2A title game
entries.summary: <p>CHAMPAIGN—Peoria Manual senior Reggie Postlewaite ripped the ball right out of the hands of a Tolono Unity player at one point in the second half of the Rams' 62-57 win against Tolono Unity in the Class 2A semifinals at State Farm Center on <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/high-school-basketball/scores/2026/03/09/ihsa-boys-basketball-state-finals-scores-illinois" >Thursday</a>. </p><p>"We have a lot of guys on the roster that won’t let us lose," Manual coach Marvin Jordan said. "Reggie is one of those guys. It shows up each and every game. You can’t ask for a better player. He is everything a coach asks for."</p><p>Postlewaite filled up the stat sheet with 13 points, eight rebounds, seven steals, and six assists. </p><p>"It feels amazing to be back [at the state finals]," Postlewaite said. "We came up short last year. We were determined we were going to come back and get first place this year. My emotions are everywhere."</p><p>Manual (24-9) will face Quincy Notre Dame in the 2A championship game on Saturday. </p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-570000" name="image-570000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/33ecb30/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3314x1860+0+0/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc8%2F1d%2F2ee83c5143e48c57484907c51fd0%2Fob-cst-031326-002.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/6b9bbd4/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3314x1860+0+0/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc8%2F1d%2F2ee83c5143e48c57484907c51fd0%2Fob-cst-031326-002.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/27ddda5/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3314x1860+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc8%2F1d%2F2ee83c5143e48c57484907c51fd0%2Fob-cst-031326-002.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="Peoria Manual’s Tahj Tolliver (2) moves the ball against Unity’s Brayden Henry (5) during the 2A semifinal game at State Farm Center. " srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/27ddda5/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3314x1860+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc8%2F1d%2F2ee83c5143e48c57484907c51fd0%2Fob-cst-031326-002.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/7b00d43/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3314x1860+0+0/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc8%2F1d%2F2ee83c5143e48c57484907c51fd0%2Fob-cst-031326-002.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/27ddda5/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3314x1860+0+0/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc8%2F1d%2F2ee83c5143e48c57484907c51fd0%2Fob-cst-031326-002.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Peoria Manual’s Tahj Tolliver (2) moves the ball against Unity’s Brayden Henry (5) during the 2A semifinal game at State Farm Center.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Kirsten Stickney/For the Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>"It was a hard fought game and we battled," Jordan said. "That is pretty much who we have been all year. These guys put in the work every day and believed in the plan we had."</p><p>Unity (33-3) led 47-46 after a free throw by senior Coleton Langendorf with 5:07 to play. </p><p>The Rams responded with a 10-0 burst to take control. And shot 8-for-10 from the free-throw line in the final minute to seal the win. </p><p>Joshua Humbles led Manual with 19 points and Tahj Tolliver added 13 points. </p><p>Dane Eisenmenger led Unity with 14 points and 11 rebounds and Langendorf finished with 13 points. </p>
entries.author: Michael O'Brien

entries.title: Farragut's unlikely run to state ends in 2A semifinals
entries.summary: <p>CHAMPAIGN — Farragut was once the greatest show Illinois high school basketball had seen. But the days of Kevin Garnett and Ronnie Fields are long gone.</p><p>Admirals coach William ‘‘Wolf’’ Nelson struggled to keep his program alive in the final years of his coaching career, getting by with 10 or 11 players and no sophomore team. One of Nelson’s former star players, Emmanuel Little, took over as coach three years ago, and Nelson became athletic director.</p><p>Farragut’s appearance in the Class 2A state finals is a testament to the love and care Nelson and Little have for their school. No one involved in high school basketball believed the Admirals would earn a state trophy this season, but they will play for third place after losing to Quincy Notre Dame 64-45 in the semifinals <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/high-school-basketball/scores/2026/03/09/ihsa-boys-basketball-state-finals-scores-illinois" target="_blank" >Thursday</a> at the State Farm Center.</p><p>‘‘I’m just excited about these boys,’’ Little said. ‘‘They are exceptional on and off the court. A lot of them are having emotional tears, but they should be happy. Sad is when you mourn something bad. They should be happy tears.’’</p><p><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/high-school-basketball/2026/02/20/chicago-sun-times-all-city-high-school-basketball-team-2026" target="_blank" >All-City</a> senior Latrell Kelley, who led Farragut (13-15) to upset playoff victories against Phillips and Crane, finished with 21 points.</p><p>‘‘It was a long season,’’ Kelley said. ‘‘We had a tough road, and a lot of people doubted us. We prevailed. I take pride in that, but I want to win.’’</p><p>‘‘We knew that [Kelley] was the main player and that if we shut him down they would have a hard time scoring,’’ Quincy Notre Dame’s Beau Eftink said. ‘‘So whenever he came off screens or went away from us, we doubled him.’’</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Kirsten Stickney/For the Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>Kelley’s three-pointer with 4:31 left in the third quarter cut the Raiders’ lead to 32-30. Then everything fell apart for the Admirals.</p><p>Quincy Notre Dame (32-1) took control by forcing a flurry of turnovers to start a 25-4 run.</p><p></p><p>‘‘It happened on the defensive end,’’ Raiders coach Greg Altmix said. ‘‘You get pretty good shots when you get steals and turnovers. When you have a team that is willing to buy in on the defensive end, that is huge.’’</p><p></p><p>Deangelo Embry added nine points and Terez Terrell eight for Farragut. Jace Allensworth led Quincy Notre Dame with 16 points. The Raiders will face Peoria Manual in the Class 2A title game Saturday.</p><p></p><p>‘‘It’s been a tough road, coming in knowing what we have,’’ Little said. ‘‘They say you only need five on the floor, so that is what we have been doing. Sometimes it gets to you, but a lot of times it doesn’t. I’m truly happy about our guys. Farragut is still here. Never dropped out of the Red-West.’’</p>
entries.author: Michael O'Brien

entries.title: Why was Tuesday's record-breaking hail so huge?
entries.summary: <p>A massive hailstone from Tuesday's storm most likely set a new state record. </p><p>Victor Gensini, a meteorologist at Northern Illinois University, was in Kankakee following the <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/weather/2026/03/11/2-killed-after-storms-bring-at-least-4-twisters-possible-record-setting-hail-in-illinois-and-indiana" target="_blank" >supercell storm that caused at least four tornadoes</a> that tore through homes and killed an Indiana couple. </p><p>Gensini collected a hailstone and brought it back to his lab at Northern Illinois University, where he studies severe weather and climate. He measured it Thursday afternoon. By his calculations, it's 7.125 inches wide, 16 inches around and weighs just over a pound — 16.5 ounces.</p><p>Before Tuesday, the largest hailstone found in Illinois measured 4.75 inches in diameter. </p><p>"It didn't just break the record, but it shattered the record," Gensini said. "We've never seen anything like this since we've been recording hail, which started in the mid-1950s." </p><p>If, that is, Gensini's measurements are verified by a state meteorologist visiting his lab on Monday.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-7b0000" name="image-7b0000"></a> <picture data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Victor Gensini at Northern Illinois University measures what he expects will be confirmed as the largest one found in Illinois.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Mark Black/For the Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>While massive hailstones are unusual, hailstorms are quite common, Gensini said, especially this time of year,.</p><p>Roughly 3,000 to 5,000 hailstorms occur nationwide every year, and "the Chicago area sees its fair share of them," Gensini said. </p><p>Here's what to know about hailstorms, how they form and whether those hailstones are going to get any bigger. </p><h3>How does a hailstorm form? </h3><p>As with any other severe weather, Gensini says a hailstorm requires four atmospheric ingredients: <br></p><ul class="rte2-style-ul" id="rte-87bae6f2-1e61-11f1-bb8c-33e307bbfbf7"><li>shear (the change in wind speed and/or direction over short distance)</li><li>lift (air moving upwards)</li><li>instability (warm air from the ground rising and meeting cold air up in the atmosphere) </li><li>moisture (humidity in the air)</li></ul><p>And while Lake Michigan regularly influences the Chicago region's weather, it doesn't make much of a difference when it comes to hail since the stones form far up in the atmosphere, Gensini said. </p><h3>What impacts a hailstone’s size? </h3><p>Most hailstones are small, pebble-sized chunks of ice, Gensini said. But it's challenging to track the average size of a hailstone because they begin to melt as soon as they touch the ground. </p><p>That vertical blast of air is especially key for determining how big a hailstone becomes, he says.</p><p>The stronger the upward air flow, the longer the hail hangs in the atmosphere before falling to the ground, Gensini says. That allows the stones to grow larger and collide with other stones. That collision is also why some hailstones are oddly shaped and others are more round. </p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-cb0000" name="image-cb0000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/6ea9c86/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3400x1908+0+126/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F64%2F8f%2F49c5936d4c5fbf46c50f90680b48%2Fhailfaq-260322-11.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/fe92e02/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3400x1908+0+126/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F64%2F8f%2F49c5936d4c5fbf46c50f90680b48%2Fhailfaq-260322-11.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/344fe76/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3400x1908+0+126/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F64%2F8f%2F49c5936d4c5fbf46c50f90680b48%2Fhailfaq-260322-11.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="Victor Gensini weighs a large hailstone in his lab at Northern Illinois University. Gensini found the hailstone near Kankakee after storms on Tuesday, March 10, 2026." srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/344fe76/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3400x1908+0+126/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F64%2F8f%2F49c5936d4c5fbf46c50f90680b48%2Fhailfaq-260322-11.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/0b9628f/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3400x1908+0+126/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F64%2F8f%2F49c5936d4c5fbf46c50f90680b48%2Fhailfaq-260322-11.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/344fe76/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3400x1908+0+126/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F64%2F8f%2F49c5936d4c5fbf46c50f90680b48%2Fhailfaq-260322-11.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Victor Gensini weighs a large hailstone in his lab at Northern Illinois University. Gensini found the hailstone near Kankakee after storms on Tuesday. </p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Mark Black/For the Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p></p><h3>Is climate change making hail worse?</h3><p>As with other extreme weather, there isn't a simple answer, Gensini said. </p><p>"In an event like this week's storm, climate change certainly played a role," Gensini said. "But as a scientist, I'm like, well, how much of a role —2% of a role, 5% of a role, 10% of a role? Those are questions that are incredibly difficult to answer." </p><p>Gensini and his colleagues <a class="Link" href="https://www.nature.com/articles/s41612-024-00728-9" target="_blank" >published a paper in 2024</a> exploring climate change's future impacts on hail. </p><p>They found that since climate change is shown to cause stronger upward blasts of air, it's likely that larger hailstones will become more normal. But because of warming temperatures, smaller hailstones will become less frequent because they'll melt before they hit the ground. </p><p>"The science currently is very immature," Gensini said. "We have a lot of work to do in terms of attributing climate change to specific tornado or hailstorm events." </p><h3>Will hail become more destructive? </h3><h3></h3><p>What's certain, Gensini said, is future storms will cause more and more damage. </p><p>Hail already causes roughly $60 billion worth of damage in the U.S. each year, Gensini said. </p><p>"Regardless of the impact of climate change, I can guarantee you that we're going to see more hailstorm disasters in the future," he said. "It has really nothing to do with climate change. It has everything to do with the fact that ... our cities are growing larger and the bullseyes on the dartboard, so to speak, are growing larger as sprawl continues." </p>
entries.author: Kaitlin Washburn

entries.title: Big Ten's big boppers — Michigan, Nebraska, Michigan State and Illinois — enter the postseason chat on Friday
entries.summary: <p>The <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/college-sports" target="_blank" >Big Ten men’s basketball</a> tournament lumbered on Thursday, the third day of <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/college-sports/2026/03/08/big-ten-tournament-illinois-michigan-nebraska-purdue-united-center-chicago" target="_blank" >the league’s first-ever six-day, 18-team postseason spectacle</a>, all of it at the United Center.</p><p>On Day 3, a whopping 12 teams remained in the picture. Was this a conference tournament or a race for one of the area’s open Congressional seats?</p><p>But that’s life in the largest conference in the land. And for March Madness lovers, life is about to get a lot better on Friday, when the Big Ten’s top four seeds make their first appearances and the postseason begins cooking with grease on the West Side.</p><p>The Big Ten also is the only conference in college basketball with four teams ranked among the top 11 in the country. Top-seeded Michigan is a 29-2 monster that won 19 of 20 league games. The next three, in order — Nebraska (26-5), Michigan State (25-6) and Illinois (24-7) — each finished 15-5 in league play.</p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/college-sports/2026/03/11/northwestern-wildcats-indiana-hoosiers-big-ten-tournament-purdue-march-madness" >Northwestern bounces Indiana 74-61 in Big Ten tournament second-round game to earn another shot at Purdue</a> </li> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/college-sports/2026/03/10/big-ten-tournament-opening-night-northwestern-penn-state-indiana-chris-collins-nick-martinelli" >On dud of a Big Ten Tournament opening night, at least Northwestern won a game</a> </li> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/sports-media/2026/03/12/bruce-weber-big-ten-network-ncaa-tournament-illinois-southern-illinois-gene-keady-tex-winter-brian-barnhart" target="_blank" >Bruce Weber rekindles connection with Chicago, enjoys newfound popularity as Big Ten Network analyst</a> </li> </ul> </div> <p>But it’s Michigan that will strut into the UC with even odds to cut down the nets on Sunday and the best odds of any team, anywhere, to win the national championship. It would be the Big Ten’s first since Michigan State went all the way in 2000.</p><p>“We’ve heard a lot about this [superteam],” Wolverines coach Dusty May said. “As a staff, we felt like we had a superteam. …</p><p>“These guys, because they’re super teammates, they’ve become a superteam. Our secret sauce is how great of teammates these guys are, period.”</p><p>Having the Big Ten’s best player, Yaxel Lendeborg, and its most intimidating frontcourt — Lendeborg, 7-3 Aday Mara and bruising Illinois transfer Morez Johnson Jr. — probably thickens that sauce at least a little.</p><p>The Wolverines will face No. 8 seed Ohio State in an 11 a.m. quarterfinal, followed by Illinois against No. 5 Wisconsin in an up-for-grabs matchup at approximately 1:30. The evening session, beginning at 5:30, will pit Nebraska against Purdue in a rare case when a 2 seed is kind of an aw-shucks underdog against a 7 seed. Michigan State will get No. 6 seed UCLA or No. 14 seed Rutgers in the finale.</p><p>MSU has won the most Big Ten tournaments — six out of 28 — and seems to have a special motivation coming in after Michigan fans taunted the Spartans last weekend as the “little brother” of the state.</p><p>“I’m nobody’s damn little brother,” Spartans coach Tom Izzo said after losing that hotly contested rivalry battle, “and neither is my team.”</p><p>Nebraska coach Fred Hoiberg, the former Bulls player and coach, remains one of the best stories in college basketball along with the surprisingly outstanding team from Lincoln. Hoiberg was born there. His mother grew up there, and both parents attended Nebraska. One of his grandfathers taught sociology at the school for 30 years, and the other was the basketball coach in the 1950s and ’60s. Hoiberg’s son, Sam, is a key player on this team, and a contract extension through 2032 means Sam’s old man is simply crushing it.</p><p>Betting sites have the Huskers as a bigger longshot than Michigan, Illinois, Michigan State or Purdue (which beat Northwestern 81-68 in the third round) to win this tournament, but Hoiberg is smelling opportunity.</p><p>“I’m really confident in this group,” he said.</p><p>And then there are the Illini, who lost to Wisconsin in overtime in Champaign in the teams’ only meeting. The Illini also lost in overtime to both MSU and UCLA during a 4-4 closing stretch that also included a loss in which they were manhandled by Michigan.</p><p>Badgers guards Nick Boyd and Blackwell went off for a combined 49 points against the Illini and topped that with a combined 57 in a third-round win against Washington. Defensively, the Illini have struggled against athletic guards all season. Some close games have gotten away, too, though coach Brad Underwood oddly took offense to a local beat writer’s question about that after the regular-season finale.</p><p>Underwood’s tone was different before heading up I-57.</p><p>“We’ve got to have a carefree mindset and know that we’re a good basketball team, but we want to be the toughest, hardest-playing team out there every night,” he said. “If we do that, we give ourselves a chance.”</p>
entries.author: Steve Greenberg

entries.title: Lawyers for family of woman killed in police chase urge City Council to OK $27 million settlement
entries.summary: <p>On the eve of a Chicago City council vote, lawyers for the family of a woman who died as a result of a high speed police chase are working to salvage a settlement deal worth millions more than originally awarded.</p><p>The Council’s Finance Committee will be asked to authorize a $27 million settlement Friday with the family of Stacy Vaughn-Harrell. The family’s lawyers on Thursday urged Council members to give the OK, arguing there's new evidence that would cause the price to balloon if the case were tried again.</p><p>The 42-year-old woman and her daughter, Kimberlyn Myers, then 21, were driving home in June 2017 when they were hit by a car that was fleeing police at roughly 50 mph through a residential area in Englewood.</p><p>“My mom was a law-abiding citizen,” Myers said at a press conference Thursday.</p><p>“My mom was a giver. My mom would do anything for anyone, and I wouldn't wish this on anyone," she added. "My mom would have been here if you guys would have doneit (the chase) correctly, but you guys did it your own way, and now here it is. We have to suffer.”</p><p>Three years ago, the family was awarded $10 million at trial after Myers and Vaughn-Harrell’s husband, Henry Harrell, sued the city and the Chicago Police Department.</p><p>Then the city appealed and the appellate court ordered a new trial based on a series of legal violations.</p><p>But in preparing for the latest trial, substantial new evidence came to light. So the law department offered a $27 million settlement — “in the best interest” of Chicago taxpayers.</p><p>“It’s a huge backfire,” the family’s lawyer, Lance Northcutt said Thursday.</p><p>Video shared with media Thursday shows the officer who was driving approached Vaughn-Harrell’s sedan while saying over the radio: “Yeah they just took off. We weren’t even chasing them.” The clip is almost a minute long and at no point does an officer attempt to help Vaughn-Harrell or her daughter inside the car.</p><p>In another clip, an officer instructs Myers to try and exit the other side of the car. Myers appears to be having a hard time understanding, responding “I need to get out I need to get out.” She then topples out of the car, over her mother, with no assistance from officers.</p><p>Ald. Ray Lopez (15th) normally helps lead the charge against police-chase settlements on grounds the city is too quick to settle and the bad guys being chased should be should be held responsible.</p><p>But after attending the closed-door briefing and hearing about the video that surfaced during the appeal, Lopez said he plans to reluctantly support the $27 million settlement.</p><p>“It will be far worse for us financially than if we just settle this matter now. The deck is stacked against us when you have the potential of having six children on the stand in tears missing their mom before a jury. The heartstrings will be pulled so tightly that I can’t imagine us having any way out,” Lopez said Thursday.</p><p>“We don’t have the option of going after the owner of the car because they already settled. We don’t have the option of holding the person accountable because non-cooperative witnesses won’t tell us who the driver of this vehicle was.”</p><p></p><p></p><p></p><p></p><p>Before the chase, police pulled over a white Kia they believed involved in a shooting, though they didn’t know if the shots came from the car, the family’s attorney said at the time. A passenger got out of the car when it was pulled over, then the Kia sped off.</p><p>Police chased the Kia in an unmarked car, followed by a marked CPD car, according to the family’s trial court attorney, who contended this violated department policy requiring a marked car to lead a chase and use both lights and sirens.</p><p>The Kia ran four stop signs before crashing into Vaughn-Harrell’s car at an intersection.</p><p>Vaughn-Harrell was killed. Myers suffered serious injuries, including a concussion, a lacerated liver, and a broken collarbone requiring a plate and five screws. Vaughn-Harrell's six surviving children included three who were teenagers at the time.</p><p>Northcutt said the family remains prepared to go to trial if needed. This time around Northcutt plans to bring evidence of a wide “systemic” cover-up.</p><p>“We could conceivably be in a trial where the City of Chicago would not be able to defend on liability based on this misconduct, and if that is the case, the only thing a jury would consider is the horror show visited on the Harrell family and the damages they have sustained as a result of this senseless, preventable, tragic death,” Northcutt said.</p><p>At trial, Northcutt believes a jury would reach a verdict “far in excess of $100 million.”<br></p>
entries.author: Fran Spielman

entries.title: Win or lose the plan for the Bulls remains the same down the stretch
entries.summary: <p>LOS ANGELES – There will be no pumping of the brakes for Billy Donovan until told otherwise, and the Bulls coach has not been delivered that message.</p><p>Even with his franchise now a half a game below Milwaukee in the draft lottery standings after Thursday’s 142-130 loss to the Lakers, it’s business as usual over the final 16 regular-season games.</p><p>That was the last talk executive vice president of basketball operations Arturas Karnisovas had with Donovan, and the same one ownership had with him.</p><p>“That would be my guess,” Donovan said, when asked if he thought that mentality would continue. “If we get to a place where we are mathematically out of anything as it relates to postseason and there are guys that are dealing with some ailments or have challenges health-wise, could they say, ‘Hey, let’s get these guys right first.’ I don’t know. Everything I got from them up to this point and time is to continue to put our best foot forward.”</p><p>Easier said than done with all the injuries this roster has endured since the purge at the Feb. 5 deadline. Not that Los Angeles was about to feel bad. While the Bulls (27-39) have had a solid showing on this five-game trip, they didn’t have to face a Luke Doncic. All he did in the win for the home team was score 51 points, grab 10 rebounds and hand out nine assists.</p><p>Another reminder of what an elite player means to a lineup and what the Bulls don’t have.</p><p>But the issue wasn’t just Doncic and the other star power of LeBron James, as much as the size being a factor. The Lakers outscored the Bulls 30-16 in second-chance points.</p><p>More growing pains for a roster that continued to be short-handed and having guys thrown into different roles. Which led to Donovan also being asked about the current evaluation process. Players like Anfernee Simons, Collin Sexton and Jaden Ivey could be elsewhere next season, and while the Bulls would love to have a strong evaluation of how they would each fit in – if they want them to fit in – that hasn’t been made easy with Simons and Ivey especially, dealing with injuries.</p><p>“The Anfernee thing has made it tough to evaluate him, but he does have a large body of work, and so does Collin,” Donovan said. “Rob (Dillingham) would be a guy that you can really, really take a look at. The Jaden (Ivey) situation has been unfortunate because he played four games and now he’s out, he’s trying to rehab, but everything I’ve gotten from (the front office) is go out there and help these guys be as competitive and successful as they can be, and put the focus on winning, and I think that’s the same thing as ownership.”</p><p></p><p><b>Brotherly love</b></p><p></p><p>Before the Bulls acquired Leonard Miller from Minnesota in the Ayo Dosunmu trade package, they had his older brother Emanuel on a two-way deal.</p><p>Maybe that’s why the transition has been so easy for baby brother because of the amount of intel he was given.</p><p>“I talk to that guy every day,” Leonard said. “I talked to him about the ins and outs of the city, the program because he was here before me so I can ask him the same questions and he gives me the answers.”</p><p></p><p><b>Calming down</b></p><p></p><p>Isaac Okoro missed his second consecutive game on Thursday, after being a late scratch before the game against Golden State.</p><p>According to Donovan, the veteran has a knee issue that flares up now and then, and it decided to make the trip out west with him.</p><p>“He’s had it before,” Donovan said. “It’s probably going to take a little bit of time to make it calm down. Generally, he starts to feel better each day that goes by. I don’t think it’s a long-term thing or anything like that. He’s just got some inflammation there that they need to calm down.”</p>
entries.author: Joe Cowley

entries.title: Blackhawks complete season sweep of Mammoth as Frank Nazar keeps surging
entries.summary: <p>SALT LAKE CITY — For months, the <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/blackhawks" target="_blank" >Blackhawks</a> longed for second-year forward Frank Nazar to get hot again. Now, they’re getting their wish.</p><p>Another two-point game by Nazar helped the Hawks rally past the Mammoth 3-2 in overtime Thursday.</p><p>The victory completed an unlikely four-game season-series sweep of the likely playoff-bound Mammoth, including three wins in the last two weeks. It’s the Hawks’ first sweep against a division foe since 2016-17 against the Stars.</p><p>"Every game we play these guys is pretty intense and pretty good hockey, so it feels great to beat them every time," Connor Bedard said. "We don't like them; they don't like us. So it's a good feeling."</p><p>Both of the Hawks' cornerstone forwards recorded one goal and one assist Thursday. </p><p>Nazar's goal gave the Hawks a brief lead in the third period, then Bedard's goal decided the game in overtime. He tapped a loose puck in behind baffled Mammoth goalie Karel Vejmelka to complete a cathartic night at the Delta Center.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" > <div class="TweetUrl"> <blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">🚨CONNOR BEDARD OT WINNER🚨 <a href="https://t.co/c926Q9DneI">pic.twitter.com/c926Q9DneI</a></p>— Blackhawks on CHSN (@CHSN_Blackhawks) <a href="https://twitter.com/CHSN_Blackhawks/status/2032302831550468265?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">March 13, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script> </div> </div> </div></div><p>For Nazar, he now has eight points in his last five games, exceeding his point total from December, January and February combined. This stretch includes three multi-point outings and a dramatic overtime winner <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/blackhawks/2026/03/09/blackhawks-pay-tribute-to-troy-murray" target="_blank" >Monday</a> at the United Center.</p><p>With 32 points in 49 games this season, Nazar is now tied for third in the Hawks' scoring race and producing at a 54-point pace over 82 games, which isn't too shabby for a 22-year-old.</p><p>"[Frank's] play has warranted the opportunities he’s getting," coach Jeff Blashill said. "He’s skated hard; he’s won more puck battles. [The] big thing for him is just making sure he has the puck a lot — and when he has it, to skate with it. </p><p>"How do you have the puck? You win puck battles and then you don’t give it away. And when you have it, you've got to attack with it. He’s shown more of that skating with the puck and being a weapon with his speed."</p><h3><b>Series experience</b></h3><p>Bedard showed off his fiery side, going back and forth with Mammoth defenseman MacKenzie Weegar before staring down a loud-mouthed Mammoth fan after setting up a Tyler Bertuzzi goal. He said he sensed Monday that emotions were ramping up in this matchup.</p><p>Blashill contested that this experience didn't quite compare to a playoff series, but it did give the young Hawks a small taste of what that could be like in the future.</p><p>"They see what the pre-scouts kind of look like, stuff like that," Blashill said. "It’s a decent look at it."</p><p>Goalie Spencer Knight, returning from a three-game absence because of illness, looked extremely sharp, making 29 saves — including a couple huge ones on Clayton Keller and JJ Peterka in the third period. He said he had been "knocked out for three days [with] no moving, no eating."</p><p>Defenseman Wyatt Kaiser appeared to injure his left shoulder late in regulation. Blashill declined to clarify if he'll be available Saturday against the Golden Knights.</p><p></p><h3><b>Korchinski conundrum</b></h3><p>Blashill hasn’t shown much faith yet in defenseman Kevin Korchinski, perhaps with good reason. His lack of strength — still a problem in his third pro season — makes him a poor fit for Blashill’s defensive system.</p><p>Davidson, however, continues to defend Korchinski — whom he said has “a ton of upside, a ton of ability” — and to preach patience about his development.</p><p>Davidson said he still thinks ‘‘playing big minutes’’ in Rockford is more valuable for Korchinski than popping in and out of the Hawks’ lineup. He has made only four NHL appearances this season.</p><p>Korchinski and Ethan Del Mastro, who also has made only four NHL appearances this season, will lose their waiver exemptions this summer and presumably will need to be full-time NHL defensemen next season. It will be interesting to see whether Korchinski’s performance in training camp in September can make Blashill comfortable with that reality.</p><p>Both also are pending restricted free agents, so it will be telling to see what their second contracts look like. The deal Lukas Reichel signed in 2024 — two years at a $1.2 million salary-cap hit — might provide a blueprint.</p>
entries.author: Ben Pope

entries.title: Horoscope for Friday, March 13, 2026
entries.summary: <h2>Moon alert</h2><p>There are no restrictions to shopping or important decisions today. The moon is in Capricorn.</p><h2>Aries (March 21-April 19)</h2><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p>You should know that you make a great impression on others today, which means you can ask for what you want. Demand the advantage! Not only do you look good with Venus in your sign, but you are also charming and diplomatic. What more do you want?</p><h2>Taurus (April 20-May 20)</h2><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p>This is a great day to make travel plans — or if you can, to travel! You will also enjoy studying, learning new things and exploring opportunities in medicine, the law, publishing and the media. Basically, you want to expand your world.</p><h2>Gemini (May 21-June 20)</h2><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p>Check out banking matters and anything to do with shared property, financial negotiations and discussing the responsibilities for someone or something because you are in the driver's seat. Because you look competent and capable in the eyes of others, bosses and authority figures will help you in some way.</p><h2>Cancer (June 21-July 22)</h2><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p>Even though Venus is now at the top of your chart, creating favorable circumstances in your business and professional life, because the moon is opposite your sign, today you will have to be accommodating and be prepared to compromise with others. Romance with someone older or a mentor figure might begin.</p><h2>Leo (July 23-Aug. 22)</h2><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p>Today you want it all. If you can travel for pleasure and explore new and beautiful places, this will delight you. However, you're also prepared to work with vigor and purpose. You might even do something to boost your health. You've got all bases covered — go for it!</p><h2>Virgo (Aug. 23-Sept. 22)</h2><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p>Gifts, goodies and favors from others can come your way today. Meanwhile, romance will be particularly passionate and affectionate. This is a great day to socialize. Enjoy sports and fun activities with kids. Make time for pleasure and socializing with others.</p><h2>Libra (Sept. 23-Oct. 22)</h2><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p>You can accomplish a lot today because you have the energy and drive. Furthermore, Mercury retrograde will help you finish old business and wrap up loose details. You will be particularly effective if you are working from home or dealing with a home business.</p><h2>Scorpio (Oct. 23-Nov. 21)</h2><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p>This is a great day for the arts, show business and anything to do with sports and the entertainment world. You feel competitive and energetic. Furthermore, you will express yourself with energy and clarity. People want to hear what you have to say.</p><h2>Sagittarius (Nov. 22-Dec. 21)</h2><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p>You might buy something today, invest in real estate or do something to shore up and secure your home and family situation. It's also a good day to tackle home repairs that have needed attention. Be patient with family members to keep the peace.</p><h2>Capricorn (Dec. 22-Jan. 19)</h2><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p>Today the moon is in your sign, dancing beautifully with the sun, boosting all your communications with others, making them positive, enthusiastic and proactive.
